title: Wahlenbergia subaphylla
taxonomy: Wahlenbergia subaphylla is a species of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae. This family is commonly known as the bellflower family. The Campanulaceae family is a part of the larger clade called the asterids within the angiosperms. The clade asterids also includes other families such as the goodeniaceae and the scrophulariaceae. Wahlenbergia subaphylla's classification is therefore: kingdom Plantae, clade angiosperms, clade asterids, order Asterales, family Campanulaceae.

parts: Wahlenbergia subaphylla, also known as the Cape bluebells, is a plant native to South Africa. intro: Wahlenbergia subaphylla, also known as the 'False Pimpernel', is a perennial herbaceous plant native to the mountainous regions of southern Africa. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in treating various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Wahlenbergia subaphylla include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a common ingredient in herbal remedies for various health conditions.
